devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । स्वायम्भुवस्येह गुरो वंशोऽयं विस्तराच्छ्रुतः । यत्र विश्वसृजां सर्गो मनूनन्यान्वदस्व नः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca svāyambhuvasyeha guro vaṃśo yatra viśvasṛjāṃ sargo manūnanyānvadasva naḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) requested:
Here (in the 7th book) I have heard from you, in details, the history of the dynasty of Svāyambhuva Manu in which were born the progenitors of the universe like Marīci and others and their progeny. Now relate to us, Oh preceptor, the history of other Manus[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Anvitārthaprakāśikā. adds:
v.l. the second half of this verse:
atra dharmās'ca vividhās'cātur varṇyā'śritāḥ subhāḥ / [?]
Here various courses of righteous and auspicious duties pertaining to four varṇas are described.

devanagari: मन्वन्तरे हरेर्जन्म कर्माणि च महीयसः । गृणन्ति कवयो ब्रह्मंस्तानि नो वद शृण्वताम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: manvantare harerjanma karmāṇi ca mahīyasaḥ gṛṇanti kavayo brahmaṃstāni no vada śṛṇvatām
translation: Oh Brahman! Be pleased to relate to us who are (eagerly) listening, the history of all those Manu-epochs (wherein) wherever the incarnations and great exploits of Hari, the greatest of the great, are sung by the wise.

devanagari: यद्यस्मिन्नन्तरे ब्रह्मन्भगवान्विश्वभावनः । कृतवान्कुरुते कर्ता ह्यतीतेऽनागतेऽद्य वा ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyasminnantare brahmanbhagavānviśvabhāvanaḥ kṛtavānkurute kartā hyatīte

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Do you also tell (in details), what Lord Hari, the creator and protector of the universe has accomplished in the past Manvantara, is doing in the present one, and will work in the Manu-epochs to come, Oh Brahman.

devanagari: श्रीऋषिरुवाच । मनवोऽस्मिन्व्यतीताः षट्कल्पे स्वायम्भुवादयः । आद्यस्ते कथितो यत्र देवादीनां च सम्भवः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīṛṣiruvāca manavo ādyaste kathito yatra devādīnāṃ ca sambhavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The sage (Śuka) replied:
In this current Kalpa (—Brahmā’s day), six Manu- epochs beginning with Svāyambhuva have elapsed. Of these, the first Manvantara (Svāyambhuva) has been described to you, along with the creation of gods and other creatures, during this period.

devanagari: आकूत्यां देवहूत्यां च दुहित्रोस्तस्य वै मनोः । धर्मज्ञानोपदेशार्थं भगवान्पुत्रतां गतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: ākūtyāṃ devahūtyāṃ ca duhitrostasya vai manoḥ dharmajñānopadeśārthaṃ bhagavānputratāṃ gataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: The Lord incarnated as (Yajña) the son of (Svāyambhuva) Manu’s daughter Akūti to enlighten her in the righteous path and as (Kapila) the son of (the same) Manu’s daughter Devahūti for imparting spiritual wisdom.

devanagari: कृतं पुरा भगवतः कपिलस्यानुवर्णितम् । आख्यास्ये भगवान्यज्ञो यच्चकार कुरूद्वह ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtaṃ purā bhagavataḥ kapilasyānuvarṇitam ākhyāsye bhagavānyajño yaccakāra kurūdvaha

translation: I have previously (in Skandha III) narrated to you the work done by venerable Kapila. I shall (now) relate to you, Oh Chief of Kurus, what the glorious Lord Yajña did.

devanagari: विरक्तः कामभोगेषु शतरूपापतिः प्रभुः । विसृज्य राज्यं तपसे सभार्यो वनमाविशत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: viraktaḥ kāmabhogeṣu śatarūpāpatiḥ prabhuḥ visṛjya rājyaṃ tapase sabhāryo vanamāviśat

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]

translation: Being indifferent and unattached to sensual enjoyment, and having abdicated his kingship, Lord Svāyambhuva Manu, the spouse of Śatarūpā, entered the forest, along with his queen, to practice penance.

devanagari: सुनन्दायां वर्षशतं पदैकेन भुवं स्पृशन् । तप्यमानस्तपो घोरमिदमन्वाह भारत ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: sunandāyāṃ varṣaśataṃ padaikena bhuvaṃ spṛśan tapyamānastapo ghoramidamanvāha bhārata

translation: Touching the ground with one foot (standing on one leg) for one hundred years, on the bank of the Sunandā, he practised terribly austere penance, Oh Bhārata, and chanted the following (prayer:).

devanagari: श्रीमनुरुवाच । येन चेतयते विश्वं विश्वं चेतयते न यम् । यो जागर्ति शयानेऽस्मिन्नायं तं वेद वेद सः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīmanuruvāca yena cetayate viśvaṃ viśvaṃ cetayate na yam yo jāgarti śayāne

translation:
Manu said:[2]
A). With his vision uninterrupted, this Vāsudeva dwells in all bodies. He does not perceive the (evolution, continuance and dissolution of) universe with the help of another. He is the cause of the (sentient and non-sentient) universe. Hence his vision (and power of controlling from within).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
The next verses are based ou Upaniṣadic texts and are known as Mantropaniṣad.

devanagari: आत्मावास्यमिदं विश्वं यत्किञ्चिज्जगत्यां जगत् । तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्य स्विद्धनम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ātmāvāsyamidaṃ viśvaṃ yatkiñcijjagatyāṃ jagat tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasya sviddhanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: [3] By him the universe is endowed with consciousness, but unto whom (none in) the universe (can) impart consciousness. He remains awake (as a witness) while the universe is in sleep (even during Pralaya). No creatures can know him but he knows all. (He is the ātman).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
It is the Sun which prompted by Hari gives light and heat to the universe, and from which, tapas, the root cause of all power becomes possible. Even the Sun does not know Lord Hari who remains awake while it (the Sun) sets. But Hari knows it fully—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: यं पश्यति न पश्यन्तं चक्षुर्यस्य न रिष्यति । तं भूतनिलयं देवं सुपर्णमुपधावत ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yaṃ paśyati na paśyantaṃ cakṣuryasya na riṣyati taṃ bhūtanilayaṃ devaṃ suparṇamupadhāvata
translation: Resort to the self-illuminating Lord who is the abode (the Inner Controller) of all beings and yet unconcerned and aloof from them like Suparṇa (or the bird of beautiful wings, the symbol of Ātman, the companion of jīva in Upaniṣads like the Muṇḍaka 3.1.1), whom though he sees all, no eye (created being) can see (as he is beyond sense-perception), and whose vision (knowledge) never vanishes, (as he is knowledge incarnate).

devanagari: न यस्याद्यन्तौ मध्यं च स्वः परो नान्तरं बहिः । विश्वस्यामूनि यद्यस्माद्विश्वं च तदृतं महत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: na yasyādyantau madhyaṃ ca svaḥ paro nāntaraṃ bahiḥ viśvasyāmūni yadyasmādviśvaṃ ca tadṛtaṃ mahat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The attributes like beginning, middle and the end cannot be predicated of him (being present in all), he has none that is his own or as belonging to another. (Being all-pervading) the distinction of ‘in-ness’ and ‘out-ness’ is not-applicable to him. All these stages (of creation, subsistence and destruction) are attributable to the universe which is his manifestation. Hence, he is the Reality, the supreme truth, the great Perfect Brahman.

devanagari: स विश्वकायः पुरुहूतईशः सत्यः स्वयंज्योतिरजः पुराणः । धत्तेऽस्य जन्माद्यजयात्मशक्त्या तां विद्ययोदस्य निरीह आस्ते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa viśvakāyaḥ puruhūtaīśaḥ satyaḥ svayaṃjyotirajaḥ purāṇaḥ dhatte
translation: He (the Supreme Being) has the universe as his body (or he contains the universe within himself at the time of the Deluge) He has innumerable designations. He is the Absolute Ruler, the Supreme Truth, self-effulgent, uncreated, eternal ancient Being. By his (Māyā) Potency, which is also uncreated, he brings about the creation (sustenance and destruction) of this (universe). By his eternal knowledge and wisdom, he sets aside Māyā and remains actionless (in his original state as Brahman).

devanagari: अथाग्रे ऋषयः कर्माणीहन्तेऽकर्महेतवे । ईहमानो हि पुरुषः प्रायोऽनीहां प्रपद्यते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: athāgre ṛṣayaḥ karmāṇīhante īhamāno hi puruṣaḥ prāyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yam class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb]

translation: (As the Lord initiates action e.g. creation of the universe etc. just to return to actionlessness) so the sages wish to undertake the religious duties with the object of attaining to actionlessness[7] or Liberation (Mokṣa). For, a person who performs actions (dedicating them to the Lord) generally attains to Mokṣa (lit. stage of actionlessness).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
v.l. Karma-hantave: for the destruction of karmas.

devanagari: ईहते भगवानीशो न हि तत्र विसज्जते । आत्मलाभेन पूर्णार्थो नावसीदन्ति येऽनु तम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: īhate bhagavānīśo na hi tatra visajjate ātmalābhena pūrṇārtho nāvasīdanti ye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sad class 1 verb]

translation: The Supreme Lord of glorious powers engages him- self in acts, but is not attached to them, for through realization (of the blissful nature) of his Self, he has all his objects achieved. Hence, those who follow him do not come to grief.

devanagari: तमीहमानं निरहङ्कृतं बुधं निराशिषं पूर्णमनन्यचोदितम् । नॄन्शिक्षयन्तं निजवर्त्मसंस्थितं प्रभुं प्रपद्येऽखिलधर्मभावनम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tamīhamānaṃ nirahaṅkṛtaṃ budhaṃ nirāśiṣaṃ pūrṇamananyacoditam nṝnśikṣayantaṃ nijavartmasaṃsthitaṃ prabhuṃ prapadye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ īh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ īh class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

[nominative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

translation: I (Svāyambhuva Manu) take shelter in the Almighty Lord who (out of grace) engages himself in activities (such as creation etc. of the universe or follows the path of Veda in his incarnations as Rāma, Kṛṣṇa etc.) yet is absolutely free from egotism as he is full of wisdom. Being Perfect, he is above all cravings (for blessings etc). Being himself the Master, he is not impelled by others; he is yet steadfast in the path of action (according to the incarnation taken by him) as he has to set an example to people and he is the exponent of the path of righteousness

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति मन्त्रोपनिषदं व्याहरन्तं समाहितम् । दृष्ट्वासुरा यातुधाना जग्धुमभ्यद्रवन्क्षुधा ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti mantropaniṣadaṃ vyāharantaṃ samāhitam dṛṣṭvāsurā yātudhānā jagdhumabhyadravankṣudhā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Seeing him thus repeating the Mantropaniṣad (the mystical prayer based on Upaniṣadic texts) in meditation, Asuras and Rākṣasas fell upon him to devour him out of hunger.

devanagari: तांस्तथावसितान्वीक्ष्य यज्ञः सर्वगतो हरिः । यामैः परिवृतो देवैर्हत्वाशासत्त्रिविष्टपम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃstathāvasitānvīkṣya yajñaḥ sarvagato hariḥ yāmaiḥ parivṛto devairhatvāśāsattriviṣṭapam
translation: The all-pervading Hari in the form of Yajña, seeing them determined to kill Manu, killed them with the help of gods known as Yāma (who were his sons), and ruled over the Heaven (as the Indra thereof).[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Bhāgavata Purāṇa 12.7.15 mentions that each Manvantara is presided over by a Manu as its head, a group of gods (the Manu’s sons), an Indra, seven sages and an incarnation of Lord Viṣṇu. Thus in the first Manvantara Svāyambhuva (the son of Brahmā) was the Manu; the gods were known as Yāma; Yajña was the incarnation of Lord Viṣṇu who officiated as Indra also; and the seven sages were Marīci, Atri, Aṅgirā, Pulaha, Kratu, Pulastya and Vasiṣṭha.

devanagari: स्वारोचिषो द्वितीयस्तु मनुरग्नेः सुतोऽभवत् । द्युमत्सुषेणरोचिष्मत्प्रमुखास्तस्य चात्मजाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: svārociṣo dvitīyastu manuragneḥ suto dyumatsuṣeṇarociṣmatpramukhāstasya cātmajāḥ
translation: The second Manu was Svārociṣa, the son of Fire God. He had Dyumat, Suṣeṇa, Rociṣmat as his prominent sons.

devanagari: तत्रेन्द्रो रोचनस्त्वासीद्देवाश्च तुषितादयः । ऊर्जस्तम्भादयः सप्त ऋषयो ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tatrendro rocanastvāsīddevāśca tuṣitādayaḥ ūrjastambhādayaḥ sapta ṛṣayo brahmavādinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: There Rocana was the Indra, and the gods were (his sons) Tuṣita, and others (Toṣa, Pratoṣa, Santoṣa, Bhadra, Śānti, Iḍaspati, Idhma, Kavi, Vibhu, Svahna, Sudeva, Rocana as listed in the fourth Skandha). The seven sages, well-versed in the Vedas and the knowledge of Brahman, were Ūrja (son of Vasiṣṭha), Stambha (son of Kāśyapa) and others (prāṇa, Bṛhaspati, Datta (son of Atri), Atri, Cyavana as enumerated in the Harivaṃśa).

devanagari: ऋषेस्तु वेदशिरसस्तुषिता नाम पत्न्यभूत् । तस्यां जज्ञे ततो देवो विभुरित्यभिविश्रुतः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣestu vedaśirasastuṣitā nāma patnyabhūt tasyāṃ jajñe tato devo vibhurityabhiviśrutaḥ

translation: From the sage Veda-Śiras and through his wife Tuṣitā by name, the Lord Viṣṇu was born and was well known as Vibhu.

devanagari: अष्टाशीतिसहस्राणि मुनयो ये धृतव्रताः । अन्वशिक्षन्व्रतं तस्य कौमारब्रह्मचारिणः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭāśītisahasrāṇi munayo ye dhṛtavratāḥ anvaśikṣanvrataṃ tasya kaumārabrahmacāriṇaḥ
translation: Eighty-eight thousand sages who have undertaken the vow of austerities, followed his vow of celibacy, as he was celibate since boyhood.

devanagari: तृतीय उत्तमो नाम प्रियव्रतसुतो मनुः । पवनः सृञ्जयो यज्ञ होत्राद्यास्तत्सुता नृप ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛtīya uttamo nāma priyavratasuto manuḥ pavanaḥ sṛñjayo yajña hotrādyāstatsutā nṛpa

translation: The third Manu was by name Uttama, the son of Priyavrata. He had sons prominent among whom were Pāvana, Sṛñjaya, Yajñahotra, Oh King.

devanagari: वसिष्ठतनयाः सप्त ऋषयः प्रमदादयः । सत्या वेदश्रुता भद्रा देवा इन्द्रस्तु सत्यजित् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: vasiṣṭhatanayāḥ sapta ṛṣayaḥ pramadādayaḥ satyā vedaśrutā bhadrā devā indrastu satyajit
translation: In that Manvantara, Pramada and other seven sons of Vasiṣṭha were the seven sages. The three groups of gods were Satya, Vedaśruta and Bhadra, and Satyajit was the Indra.

devanagari: धर्मस्य सूनृतायां तु भगवान्पुरुषोत्तमः । सत्यसेन इति ख्यातो जातः सत्यव्रतैः सह ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmasya sūnṛtāyāṃ tu bhagavānpuruṣottamaḥ satyasena iti khyāto jātaḥ satyavrataiḥ saha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ khyā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ khyā class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ khyā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ khyā class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ khyā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ khyā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: The glorious Supreme Lord was born from Dharma through his wife Sunṛtā and became known as Satyasena. Satyavrata gods were also born along with him.

devanagari: सोऽनृतव्रतदुःशीलानसतो यक्षराक्षसान् । भूतद्रुहो भूतगणांश्चावधीत्सत्यजित्सखः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: so bhūtadruho bhūtagaṇāṃścāvadhītsatyajitsakhaḥ
translation: As a friend of the Indra Satyajit, he killed wicked Yakṣas and Rākṣasas who practised hypocrisy and were of bad character, and also the hosts of evil spirits who harmed living beings.

devanagari: चतुर्थ उत्तमभ्राता मनुर्नाम्ना च तामसः । पृथुः ख्यातिर्नरः केतुरित्याद्या दश तत्सुताः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: caturtha uttamabhrātā manurnāmnā ca tāmasaḥ pṛthuḥ khyātirnaraḥ keturityādyā daśa tatsutāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]

translation: The fourth Manu was by name Tāmasa, and was the brother of Uttama. He had ten sons such as Pṛthu, Khyāti, Nara, Ketu and others.

devanagari: सत्यका हरयो वीरा देवास्त्रिशिख ईश्वरः । ज्योतिर्धामादयः सप्त ऋषयस्तामसेऽन्तरे ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: satyakā harayo vīrā devāstriśikha īśvaraḥ jyotirdhāmādayaḥ sapta ṛṣayastāmase
translation: In the Manvantara called Tāmasa, the gods were of three groups, Satyaka, Hari and Vīra, and the Lord of the Heavens was Triśikha, and the seven sages were Jyotis, Dhāma and others (namely Pṛthu, Kāvya, Caitra, Agni, Dhanada, and Pīvara according to Harivaṃśa).

devanagari: देवा वैधृतयो नाम विधृतेस्तनया नृप । नष्टाः कालेन यैर्वेदा विधृताः स्वेन तेजसा ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: devā vaidhṛtayo nāma vidhṛtestanayā nṛpa naṣṭāḥ kālena yairvedā vidhṛtāḥ svena tejasā

translation: The gods were called Vaidhṛti, as they were the sons of Vidhṛti, Oh King. They, with their own intellectual power, retained the Vedas which were lost in the course of time.

devanagari: तत्रापि जज्ञे भगवान्हरिण्यां हरिमेधसः । हरिरित्याहृतो येन गजेन्द्रो मोचितो ग्रहात् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpi jajñe bhagavānhariṇyāṃ harimedhasaḥ harirityāhṛto yena gajendro mocito grahāt

translation: In that Manvantara was born Lord Hari from Harimedhas through Hariṇī, and he rescued the big elephant from the grip of an alligator.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । बादरायण एतत्ते श्रोतुमिच्छामहे वयम् । हरिर्यथा गजपतिं ग्राहग्रस्तममूमुचत् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca bādarāyaṇa etatte śrotumicchāmahe vayam hariryathā gajapatiṃ grāhagrastamamūmucat

[nominative single from √ gras class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ gras class 1 verb]

translation:
The King Requested:
Oh son of Vyāsa, we are desirous of hearing from you how Hari delivered the abovementioned King of Elephants seized by the alligator.

devanagari: तत्कथासु महत्पुण्यं धन्यं स्वस्त्ययनं शुभम् । यत्र यत्रोत्तमश्लोको भगवान्गीयते हरिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tatkathāsu mahatpuṇyaṃ dhanyaṃ svastyayanaṃ śubham yatra yatrottamaśloko bhagavāngīyate hariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb]

translation: Wherever the Supreme Lord Hari of most hallowed renown is sung, that story is extremely sacred, praiseworthy, auspicious and contributory to our welfare.

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । परीक्षितैवं स तु बादरायणिः प्रायोपविष्टेन कथासु चोदितः । उवाच विप्राः प्रतिनन्द्य पार्थिवं मुदा मुनीनां सदसि स्म शृण्वताम...
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvāca parīkṣitaivaṃ sa tu bādarāyaṇiḥ prāyopaviṣṭena kathāsu coditaḥ uvāca viprāḥ pratinandya pārthivaṃ mudā munīnāṃ sadasi sma śṛṇvatām

translation:
Sūta said:
When the son of Vyāsa was urged to continue the narration by King Parīkṣit, who had determined to die of starvation, Śuka congratulated the king with delight and continued (his narration) in the assembly of sages who were listening with rapt attention, Oh Brāhmaṇas.
